findings.description: The original PDF page 1 has a two-column layout with audio troubleshooting on the left and the welcome/meeting info on the right with a cork-board background. The HTML renders these as a single column stacked vertically.
findings.evidence: PDF shows side-by-side panels (left: audio troubleshooting, right: welcome message with decorative background). HTML shows audio troubleshooting section above the welcome section in a single column.

findings.description: The cork-board textured background behind the welcome message is absent in the HTML.
findings.evidence: PDF has a brown cork-board texture behind 'Welcome to the CHPP Steering Committee Meeting'. HTML has a plain white background.

findings.description: The decorative cursive/script font for the welcome heading and date is replaced with standard font in the HTML.
findings.evidence: PDF uses an ornate script/calligraphy font for 'Welcome to the CHPP Steering Committee Meeting' and 'July 30, 2020'. HTML uses a standard sans-serif heading font.

findings.description: The green highlight box behind the chat box prompt text is absent in the HTML.
findings.evidence: PDF shows a green/mint colored box behind 'Say Hi in the chat box...' and 'Please mute'. HTML shows plain text without the colored background.

findings.description: Agenda page layout is reasonably preserved. The HTML renders the agenda as a proper table, which is functionally equivalent to the original layout.
findings.evidence: PDF shows time-topic pairs in a list-like format. HTML renders as a structured table with Time and Topic columns.

findings.description: The Coastal Habitat Protection Plan timeline slide has wildlife/nature images along the right side in the PDF that are rendered at the top of the page in the HTML, displaced from their original position next to the table.
findings.evidence: PDF shows images (egret, shellfish, pufferfish, turtle, coral reef) vertically stacked on the right side alongside the timeline table. HTML shows these same images stacked at the top of the page, above the table content.

findings.description: The timeline table header styling with blue background is absent in HTML but data integrity is preserved.
findings.evidence: PDF has blue header row and alternating row shading. HTML has a plain table with standard styling.

findings.description: Multiple h1 headings used. The document has three h1 elements: 'Welcome to the CHPP Steering Committee Meeting', 'Agenda', and 'Coastal Habitat Protection Plan'. Only one h1 should be used as the document title.
findings.evidence:
HTML contains:

Welcome to the CHPP Steering Committee Meeting

,

Agenda

,

Coastal Habitat Protection Plan

findings.description: The audio troubleshooting content and the welcome/meeting content from page 1 are presented twice — once as extracted text and once as described via the image alt text and subsequent text blocks. The welcome slide text ('Welcome to the CHPP Steering Committee Meeting', 'July 30, 2020', 'Say Hi...', 'Please mute') appears both in the image alt text context and as standalone text below it.
findings.evidence: The image alt text says 'Meeting welcome slide with audio troubleshooting instructions' and then the full text of the audio troubleshooting and welcome content is repeated as body text below the image.

findings.description: A stray '3' appears as a standalone paragraph in the Timeline section. This is the page number from the PDF slide, which should have been removed as part of cleanup.
findings.evidence:
HTML contains '

3

' between the 'Timeline' heading and the timeline table, corresponding to the slide number '3' in the PDF.

findings.description: Image alt text descriptions are reasonable but somewhat generic and may not perfectly describe all image content. The collage image alt text says 'Collage of marine life including fish and a scallop' but the PDF shows images including what appears to be an egret, clustered shellfish/oysters, a sea turtle or horseshoe crab, and a grouper fish as separate sidebar images rather than a single collage.
findings.evidence: PDF page 3 sidebar shows 4 separate stacked nature images. HTML describes them as 3 images: 'White bird wading in water', 'Collage of marine life including fish and a scallop', and 'Grouper fish underwater'.
